suites.name: @ecca/crypto
suites.desc: SHA-256, HKDF-SHA512 key derivation, AES-256-GCM encryption, Ed25519 signatures, Merkle trees, MMR, coherence roots. All via @noble audited libraries.

tests.name: returns 32 bytes for string input
returns 32 bytes for Uint8Array input
is deterministic
different inputs produce different hashes
returns 64-char hex string
accepts objects
is deterministic for objects
produces ecca:// prefixed CID with epoch
content-addressing: same content + epoch = same CID
different epoch = different CID

returns null for non-ECCA CIDs
handles CID without epoch
returns 32-byte key
is deterministic for same inputs
different stack IDs produce different keys
different epochs produce different keys
uses HKDF-SHA512
round-trips string content
round-trips binary content
produces versioned payload
different encryptions of same plaintext produce different ciphertexts
decryption with wrong key fails
decryption with wrong version throws
generates hex-encoded keypair
different calls produce different keypairs
sign + verify round trip
verify rejects wrong message
verify rejects wrong pubkey
sign accepts Uint8Array message
returns hex string
is deterministic
different leaves produce different roots
order matters
handles single leaf
handles empty array
accepts Uint8Array leaves
generates verifiable proof for each leaf
proof fails with wrong root
proof fails with wrong leaf
rejects out-of-range index
handles odd number of leaves
starts empty
append returns root hex after each insert
root changes with each append
is deterministic
snapshot captures size and peaks
peak count follows binary structure
returns hex string
is deterministic
different inputs produce different roots
covers all four chains in the hash
round-trips bytes

suites.name: @ecca/service-base
suites.desc: Fastify bootstrap, health/readiness probes, CORS, graceful shutdown. Every TypeScript service uses this foundation.

tests.name: returns a pino logger with the given name
creates a Fastify instance
registers /healthz endpoint
registers /readyz endpoint
uses cors by default
can disable cors
binds to a port
registers signal handlers without error
accepts an extra cleanup callback
is a function

suites.name: @ecca/semantic-address
suites.desc: Hierarchical content-derived addressing: 5-facet grammar (domain>entity>relation>temporal>qualifier), prefix queries, address book, NLP parser.

tests.name: produces identical output regardless of input order
normalizes case and whitespace
sorts multi-value facets
reports depth correctly
produces 32-byte addresses
same grammar + same content = same address
same grammar + different content = same prefix, different address
encodes depth correctly
produces prefix bytes equal to depth * 4
stops at first missing facet
partial prefix matches full address with same leading grammar
partial prefix does NOT match address with different domain
query by partial address returns matching entries
hex prefix query works for partial address resolution
extracts domain from natural language
extracts temporal markers
same meaning different phrasing → same normalized address

suites.name: contracts
suites.desc: Solidity smart contracts — StackIdentity, BandwidthToken, EpochAnchor, TripartiteGame, ResidueRegistry, NeedlecastRouter, QuellistTreasury, SleeveRegistry

tests.name: should have correct name and symbol for ComputeToken (337ms)
should have correct name and symbol for MemoryToken
should have 18 decimals — fine-grained cognitive capacity
should mint bandwidth to a stack — consciousness comes alive
should increase totalSupply on mint — more bandwidth in the system
should reject mint from non-minter — only the treasury can issue bandwidth
should allow owner to change the minter — treasury upgrade
should allow stack owner to authorize a sleeve runtime
should reject authorization from non-owner — you don't own that stack
should allow revoking sleeve authorization — decommission
should allow authorized sleeve to spend bandwidth
should allow stack owner to spend directly
should reject spend from unauthorized address — no stealing bandwidth
should reject overspend — insufficient bandwidth for cognitive op
should decrease totalSupply on spend — bandwidth consumed
should transfer bandwidth between stacks
should reject transfer from non-owner — can't move someone else's bandwidth
should reject transfer exceeding balance — can't overdraft consciousness
should allow owner to authorize a bridger — the needle operator
should allow owner to revoke bridger — decommissioning the needle
should reject non-owner from setting bridger — CTAC-only authority
should commit an epoch anchor with all coherence roots
should advance the head epoch pointer — time marches forward
should allow multiple epoch commits in sequence — building the timeline
should reject epoch regression — no time travel allowed
should reject same epoch re-commit — each heartbeat is unique
should reject commit from non-bridger — unauthorized needle transmission
should record timestamp — when the anchor was committed to the chain
should return zero-state for uncommitted epochs
should start with head at 0 — no epochs committed yet
should reject an anchor whose medullaHeight is not strictly greater
should track lastMedullaHeight across commits
verifyContinuity returns (true, true) when both anchors exist and roots advanced
verifyContinuity returns (false, false) for the genesis epoch
verifyContinuity returns (false, false) for an epoch with no prior anchor
verifyShardInclusion accepts a valid Merkle proof for L0
verifyShardInclusion accepts a valid Merkle proof for L3 (right-right path)
verifyShardInclusion rejects a forged proof
verifyShardInclusion returns false for an unknown epoch
verifyShardInclusion returns false for a bad shard id
should allow anyone to anchor an epoch — mining is permissionless
should store anchor data — epoch, root, miner, timestamp
should accumulate anchors — building the coherence timeline
should route a needlecast for the stack owner
should track lastAnchorOf — linking stacks to their latest needlecast point
should reject route from non-owner — can't needlecast someone else's stack
should allow owner to needlecast their own stack
should allow multiple needlecasts — consciousness can move many times
should reject epoch regression in needlecast — no going back in time
should support complete flow: mine epoch, then needlecast through it
should issue ComputeToken to a stack — processing power granted
should issue MemoryToken — expanding recall capacity
should issue SyncToken — cross-chain coherence resources
should issue RoutingToken — needlecast routing capacity
should issue ResidueToken — bounty for resolving coordination residues
should reject invalid token kind — only 5 bandwidth channels exist
should reject issuance from non-owner — Quellist principles cannot be subverted
should claim epoch rewards — equal bandwidth across all four channels
should track lastClaimEpoch — preventing double claims
should reject claim for same epoch — no double-dipping consciousness
should reject claim for earlier epoch — time regression
should allow incremental claims — epoch by epoch progression
should allow owner to change emission rate
should reject emission change from non-owner
should apply new emission rate to subsequent claims
should detect a StaleOrdering residue — routing lag artifact
should detect a SpeculativeDivergence residue — compute/memory mismatch
should detect a ReorgOrphan residue — medulla-pow chain reorg crossed anchor
should reject duplicate residue detection — each glitch is unique
should reject bounty exceeding MAX_BOUNTY — prevent economic overflow
should track residue count — how many ghosts haunt the system
should resolve a residue with valid proof — ghost exorcised
should mint ResidueToken as bounty to resolver — reward for fixing coherence
should reject proof on non-existent residue — can't fix what isn't broken
should reject double-resolution — first valid proof wins
should reject proof from non-stack-owner — impersonation prevented
should store proofHash — keccak of the resolution evidence
should default to first-valid-proof model — meritocratic resolution
should allow owner to change payout model — governance decision
should reject model change from non-owner — only governance can alter economics
should register a Human embodiment sleeve — organic body
should register an AI embodiment sleeve — digital construct
should register a Mining embodiment sleeve — proof-of-work body
should register a Memory embodiment sleeve — hippocampus storage
should store host ID — which machine runs this sleeve
should reject duplicate registration — each sleeve is unique
should reject registration from non-stack-owner — only you can clothe yourself
should add sleeve to stack's sleeve list — tracking all bodies
should decommission a sleeve — the body dies, but the stack lives on
should reject decommission of already-dead sleeve — can't kill what's dead
should reject decommission from non-owner — only you can retire your body
should allow a stack to have multiple active sleeves simultaneously
should keep decommissioned sleeves in the list — history is preserved
should return empty list for stack with no sleeves — a naked stack
should mint a cortical stack with correct identity pubkey
should assign sequential tokenIds like stack serial numbers
should emit StackMinted event — consciousness comes online
should store the Coherence Profile Vector (CPV) — cognitive personality matrix
should store Epoch Binding Curve — how fast memories decay
should reject CPV coefficient > 2.0 — no consciousness can exceed limits
should reject decay rate > 1.0 — binding cannot exceed unity
should allow owner to authorize a router contract
should allow owner to revoke router authorization
should reject non-owner from authorizing routers — only CTAC has that power
should record a needlecast from an authorized router
should reject needlecast from unauthorized sender — only sanctioned routes
should reject epoch regression — time only moves forward
should reject needlecast on non-existent stack — can't cast into void
should return zero-state for freshly minted stack
should let the referee open a new game
should reject double-open of the same game
should reject openGame from non-referee — no rogue notary
should let each party register itself with a per-epoch budget
should reject registration by non-stack-owner — only the bearer
should reject double-registration of the same party
should expose the full party roster to inspectors
should let a registered party consume compute within its budget
should let consumption accumulate up to the per-epoch cap
should revert when a single consume exceeds the per-epoch budget
should revert when accumulated consumption tips over the cap
should reset budgets per epoch — same cap applies in the next epoch
should reject consume from a party that never registered
should reject consume against an unknown game id
should reject consume with an out-of-range resource enum
should burn the underlying BandwidthToken on every consume
should fail if the underlying BandwidthToken balance is insufficient
verifyAllocationFair returns true when no party exceeds its cap
verifyAllocationFair returns true even with all parties at the exact cap
auditEpoch emits AllocationVerified(true) and returns true on a fair epoch
remainingBudget reports the unspent allowance per resource
remainingBudget for an unregistered party is 0
any inspector address can read verifyAllocationFair — view is permissionless
emits a Consumed event for every spend so inspectors can re-derive the ledger
makes overspend impossible: the cap is a hard property, not a guideline
supports many epochs of activity and remains auditable for each
